web1: Project focused on simulating a real e-commerce experience through dynamic navigation and real-time rendering.

web3: Real-time cinema booking system built with React and Firebase, focused on multi-user synchronization, concurrency control, and temporary seat reservation logic. Includes authentication, automatic reservation expiration, QR code ticket generation, and advanced time-based functionality.
web3m1: Real-time cinema booking system built with React and Firebase, inspired by production-level ticketing platforms. The application implements multi-user synchronization, concurrency control, and temporary seat reservation logic using Firestore real-time features.
web3m2: Real-time seat synchronization between multiple users
web3m3: Temporary seat locking system with automatic expiration
web3m4: Double booking prevention using Firestore transactions
web3m5: Complete ticket purchase and confirmation flow
web3m6: Digital ticket generation with QR codes
web3m7: User ticket dashboard with ticket status classification
web3m9: Modular architecture based on reusable components, hooks, and services
web3m10: Reservation system synchronized with Firestore in real time
web3m11: Advanced time-based logic for expiration and showtime validation
web3m13: Testing of hooks, timers, and reservation logic using Jest
web3m14: Concurrency control: Implemented transactions to prevent reservation conflicts between users
web3m15: Real-time synchronization: Used Firestore listeners to instantly update seat availability
web3m16: Complex time-based logic: Managed automatic expiration, countdown timers, and showtime validation
web3m17: Scalable architecture: Separated business logic using custom hooks and reusable services
web3m18: Working with real-time systems using Firebase
web3m19: Managing complex state in React applications
web3m20: Testing asynchronous logic and timers with Jest
web3m21: Designing interactive experiences inspired by real-world applications
web5: Interactive memory game built with React and Firebase, featuring authentication, real-time gameplay logic, and a global leaderboard system.
web5m1: Interactive memory game built with React and Firebase, featuring user authentication, multiple difficulty levels, dynamic categories, and a global leaderboard system.
web5m2: Users log in and try to find matching card pairs in the fewest moves possible. The game manages state in real time, plays sound effects, applies animations, and records results in a global leaderboard shared across users.
web5m4: Implementation of full memory game logic (cards, turns, and matches)
web5m7: Integration of sound effects and interactive feedback
web5m9: Data and score persistence using Firestore
web5m10: Implementation of a global leaderboard across users
web5m11: Game state management: controlling cards, turns, and validations in real time using React
web5m12: Global leaderboard: storing and retrieving scores from Firestore to display user rankings
web5m13: User experience: integrating sound effects and GSAP animations without impacting performance
web5m14: State management in interactive React applications
overviewTxt001: Pensá en Verde is a fictional ecological campaign poster designed to encourage recycling through an attractive reward. It uses a fresh, friendly, and clear aesthetic aimed at engaging a young urban audience. The design focuses on motivating immediate action by connecting recycling with a sustainable cultural event.
overviewTxt002: Asfalto is a fictional magazine dedicated to urban skateboarding culture. The cover was designed to capture the raw energy of the streets through a dynamic composition. The visual approach aims to reflect the rebellious attitude of skateboarding, connecting with a young, alternative audience passionate about movement, freedom, and self-expression.
overviewTxt003: Harry Potter-themed wedding invitation cards, created especially for a couple who are fans of the magical world. The design combines elegance with subtle references to the universe of the saga, integrating vintage-style typography, illustrations inspired by Hogwarts, and a refined color palette with warm brown accents.
overviewTxt004: Logo design inspired by a classic vinyl record for a fictional music store called Garage94.The logo combines an underground aesthetic with a retro vibe, using black and fuchsia colors. Designed to stand out on merchandise, vinyl records, and signage, it strongly represents Garage94’s rebellious and authentic identity.
themeTxt003: Magical wedding inspired by Harry Potter
themeTxt004: Brand identity for an alternative music store
